channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Survivors of child abuse at Illinois youth detention centers file more lawsuits in hopes of change
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Marcus Walker said the sexual abuse he experienced two decades ago as a teenager at an Illinois youth detention facility has haunted his life. After a staff member gave him drugs and sexually abused him, he joined a gang hoping it would protect him. It didn't.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Senator seeks watchdog inquiry into Kash Patel, alleges behind-the-scenes role in purge at FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top Democratic senator has asked the Justice Department's inspector general to investigate after he says he received information that President Donald Trump's pick to lead the FBI, Kash Patel, had been “personally directing the ongoing purge” of agents at the bureau.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Storm makes for treacherous travel as snow and ice track through the mid-Atlantic
item.description: A snowstorm blew into the mid-Atlantic states on Tuesday, causing dozens of accidents on icy roads, prompting school closures and stoking worries about possible power outages.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most US kids aren't gaining fast enough in reading and math. These schools are different
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kevin Hart will be the on-court emcee for Sunday's NBA All-Star tournament in San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Kevin Hart will be on the court at the NBA All-Star Game. The comedian and actor will be the master of ceremonies for the All-Star Game, a mini-tournament that takes place Sunday in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese spy balloon was equipped with tech from at least 5 US firms: report
item.description: A Chinese spy balloon that floated across the U.S. before being shot down off the Carolina coast in 2023 was carrying technology from at least five American companies, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hollowed-out homes. Cars entombed by mud. Unpeopled roads. Belongings reduced to dirt and debris. It all took a toll on Taylor Schenker. After Hurricane Helene last September, Schenker was upset by the deluge of images of Asheville, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: ANNA FURMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Tate, brother Tristan sued for allegedly sex trafficking American woman to foreign country
item.description: Influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been sued in Florida for allegedly sex trafficking a US woman to Romania and trying to intimidate her as a witness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murphy looks to be guiding force for Democrats in new Trump era
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) is taking a front-and-center role in the efforts to push Democrats toward becoming the party of economic populism and to challenge President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't block immigration arrests in houses of worship. Now these 27 religious groups are suing
item.description: More than two-dozen Christian and Jewish groups representing millions of Americans have filed a federal court lawsuit challenging a Trump administration move giving immigration agents more leeway to make arrests at houses of worship.
item.dc:creator: David Crary, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Jersey driver awarded $13M after state troopers mistakenly believed her stroke was sign of intoxication
item.description: A New Jersey driver was awarded nearly $13 million after she was arrested by a trooper who mistakenly believed she was drunk when she had a stroke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants stranded in Mexico try to restart life after Trump eliminates legal pathway to US
item.description: TIJUANA, Mexico (AP) — Margelis Rodriguez and her two children took selfies on their flight to Tijuana, showing off the T-shirts she had custom-made to mark what she expected to be her family's life-changing moment.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Venezuela sends 2 planes to US to return migrants, signaling a potential improvement in relations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Two Venezuelan planes flew to the United States on Monday and returned home with deported Venezuelans, signaling a possible improvement in relations between longtime diplomatic adversaries and a victory for President Donald Trump in his efforts to get more countries to take the
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hawaii court rules against insurance companies in Maui wildfire, allowing $4B settlement to proceed
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii’s Supreme Court ruled Monday that insurance companies can’t bring their own legal actions against those blamed for Maui’s catastrophic 2023 wildfire, allowing a $4 billion settlement that was on that the verge of collapse to proceed.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Election official in Arizona’s largest county signals a shift away from combating disinformation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — One of the top election officials in Arizona's largest county, which has been roiled in recent years by conspiracy theories and intimidation of election workers, is moving away from the office's previous efforts to combat misinformation.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hoarder arrested for extreme animal cruelty, as authorities discover 10 dead pets, rescue many more: police
item.description: A Long Island woman was arrested and charged with animal cruelty and other charges after a search warrant on her residence revealed unlivable conditions for dozens of animals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Researchers announce major discovery about Earth's inner core
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Researchers at the University of Southern California say they’ve unraveled the mystery of why Earth's inner core has been slowing, gradually altering the length of a day.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday. Here’s what you need to know. Starbucks The coffee chain is offering its rewards members a free tall hot or iced brewed coffee any time during “Starbucks Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has unleashed chaos by distraction upon the international community. That's no accident
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Saudis are furious. The Danes are scrambling. Colombia has backed down. Mexico and Canada stand in a purgatory between tariff wars with the US and … not. China has retaliated, launching a trade war between the economic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles deny the Chiefs a Super Bowl three-peat with dominant defense in a 40-22 rout
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Yo Philly: Jalen Hurts and Nick Sirianni are world champions. A ferocious Philadelphia Eagles defense tormented and frustrated Patrick Mahomes while Hurts made all the plays the offense needed. So much for the Kansas City Chiefs’ quest for a Super Bowl three-peat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US skier Breezy Johnson marks return from 14-month ban with a gold in world championship downhill
item.description: Her top speed of 86 mph (138.51 kph) was among the fastest ever recorded by a woman in an Alpine skiing race.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: The US stopped allowing passport gender marker changes. Here are some of the people affected
item.description: The day after President Donald Trump returned to office, Lisa Suhay took her 21-year-old daughter, Mellow, to a passport office in Norfolk, Virginia, where they live. Getting a passport for Mellow, who is transgender, was urgent.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4
